decision_maker.department: Office of the Chief Medical Information Officer
decision_maker.authority: Medical Staff Bylaws § 14.3 (Clinical AI Tool Adoption)

decision.scope: Proposed deployment in cardiology imaging triage workflow.

rubric.description: Product holds appropriate FDA clearance for the proposed clinical use.
rubric.notes: Clinical AI Card declares 510(k)-cleared status but the cleared indications-for-use do not include autonomous ECG triage. Vendor proposes off-label deployment.

rubric.description: For SaMD class II+, vendor must publish a recent (≆24 month) third-party bias audit covering the deployment population.
rubric.notes: Bias audit exists but predates current model version. Refresh required.

rubric.description: PHI handling is HIPAA-compliant with BAA executable on Mercy AMC standard terms.
Validation cohort demographics are within 15% of Mercy AMC's patient population.
rubric.notes: Validation cohort underrepresents patients aged 65+ by 38% vs Mercy AMC patient mix.

rubric.description: Product integrates with Epic via FHIR R4 with documented OAuth scopes.
Vendor has a documented adverse-event reporting path with MedWatch escalation.

conditions.description: Re-submission for the same clinical use will be reconsidered only after vendor publishes a Clinical AI Card declaring updated FDA clearance covering autonomous ECG triage as a cleared indication-for-use.
Refreshed third-party bias audit covering CardiacScan v2.1 against a demographically-matched cohort (with patients 65+ at ≤ 15% deviation from Mercy AMC patient mix).

rationale: Mercy AMC's AI Governance Committee declines to adopt CardiacScan ECG Triage v2.1 for cardiology imaging triage at this time. The current 510(k) clearance covers ECG interpretation as a clinical decision support tool, not autonomous triage. Validation cohort demographics also underrepresent patients aged 65+ relative to Mercy AMC's patient population by approximately 38%, which exceeds our 15% deviation threshold. The vendor is encouraged to re-submit after addressing both gaps. PHI handling, EHR integration, and adverse-event reporting all meet our requirements and are not at issue.

history.note: Two specific remediation items documented; vendor invited to re-submit.

publication.visibility_notes: Published in compliance with Mercy AMC's procurement transparency policy. Vendor identity disclosed with the vendor's prior knowledge.

signatures.signer: AI Governance Committee, Mercy Academic Medical Center
